zh.Witton, Northumberland, England, United: 英國英格蘭諾圖波藍德維頓 小絲
zh.lifeSummary: Roger Thornton於1420年出生在英國諾圖波藍德頓。他於1434年與Elizabeth Greystoke在英國英格蘭越可爾結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1474年在英國諾圖波藍德享年54歲。

nameMeanings.name: Thornton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish: habitational name from any of numerous places throughout England and Scotland so called, from Old English thorn ‘thorn tree’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’. The placename is most frequent in northern England, especially Yorkshire, where there are at least 16 possible sources for the surname.
Irish: Anglicized (translated) form of Gaelic Mac Sceacháin ‘son of Sceachán’ (see Skehan ).
Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Draighneáin ‘descendant of Draighneán’ (see Drennan ).

variants.name: Thornber
meanings: English: habitational name from any of the five minor places in Yorkshire called Thornber, particularly Thornber in Halton West. The placenames derive from Old English thorn ‘thorn’ + beorg ‘hill, mound’.
Irish: adopted for various Irish names containing an element meaning ‘thorn’; compare   Thornton  .

variants.name: Thorton
meanings: English: probably a shortened form of   Thornton  , or a habitational name from Thorton in Marnhull, Dorset, which has the same origin.

variants.name: Skehan
meanings: Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Sceacháin or Ó Sceacháin ‘son (or descendant) of Sceachán’, a personal name probably based on a diminutive of sceach ‘briar, thorn bush’. The Gaelic name has sometimes been ‘translated’ as Thornton .

nameMeanings.name: Roger
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French personal name, Rog(i)er, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, from hrōd ‘fame’ + gār, gēr ‘spear’. This was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain, replacing the native Old English form Hrōðgār. Roger was one of the most popular boys' names throughout the medieval period, but less so after the Reformation, though it has continued in regular use to the present day. Roger, Count of Sicily ( c.1031–1101 ), son of Tancred, recovered Sicily from the Arabs. His son, also called Roger, ruled Sicily as king, presiding over a court noted for its splendour and patronage of the arts.

variants.name: Rodger
meanings: Variant spelling of   Roger  , in part from the surname derived from the given name in the Middle Ages.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: Roger Thornton was born in 1420, in Whitton, Northumberland, England. He married Elizabeth Greystoke in 1434, in Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 2 daughters. He died in 1474, in Northumberland, England, at the age of 54.

fr.lifeSummary: Roger Thornton est né en 1420, à Whitton, Northumberland, Angleterre. Il a épousé Elizabeth Greystoke en 1434, à Everwyck, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé en 1474, à Northumberland, Angleterre, à l’âge de 54 ans.

ja.lifeSummary: Roger Thorntonは1420年にイギリスノースウンバーランドウィットオンで生まれた。1434年にイギリスイギリスヨークシュアイアでElizabeth Greystokeと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1474年にイギリスノースウンバーランドで54歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Roger Thornton(은)는 1420년 영국 노르스벌랜드 휘턴에서 태어났다. 그는 1434년 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르에서 Elizabeth Greystoke(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1474년 영국 노르스벌랜드에서 54세의 나이로 사망했다.
